proposed_hypotheses.text: The overall objective_response rate in this NSCLC cohort is substantially below 50%, indicating a low base rate that motivates looking for predictive subgroups.
Among the 77 binary features, several have a non-zero univariate association (chi-square p < 0.05) with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response rate is 0.16894 (16.89%) on n=50000 patients.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df[c], y))
analyses.result_summary: Of 77 binary features, 7 reach univariate p<0.05 with response: feature_013 (p=1.1e-33), feature_067 (p=2.0e-18), feature_006 (p=1.0e-6), feature_007 (p=5.9e-4), feature_039 (p=3.4e-3), feature_076 (p=1.9e-2), feature_112 (p=3.3e-2). Most binary features (e.g., feature_001, feature_002, feature_037, feature_102) show essentially zero association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 = 1 is associated with a LOWER objective_response rate than feature_013 = 0 (negative main effect).
feature_067 = 1 is associated with a LOWER objective_response rate than feature_067 = 0 (negative main effect).
feature_006 = 1 is associated with a HIGHER objective_response rate than feature_006 = 0 (positive main effect).

analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 (n=32365): rr=0.1540; feature_013=0 (n=17635): rr=0.1964. Risk difference -4.25 percentage points; chi-square p=1.1e-33. Strong negative association — likely a resistance biomarker.
feature_067=1 (n=12584): rr=0.1436; feature_067=0 (n=37416): rr=0.1775. Risk difference -3.39 percentage points; chi-square p=2.0e-18.
feature_006=1 (n=14258): rr=0.1819; feature_006=0 (n=35742): rr=0.1638. Risk difference +1.82 percentage points; chi-square p=1.0e-6. Positive association — candidate efficacy variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous lab/marker feature_011 is LOWER among responders than non-responders (i.e., higher feature_011 predicts non-response).
Continuous lab feature_063 is LOWER among responders than non-responders.
Of all 35 continuous features, very few besides feature_011/feature_063 will have p<0.001 association with response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011: responders 3.38, non-responders 3.93 (difference -0.55). Mann-Whitney p=1.4e-32. Strong negative association — higher values predict failure to respond.
Mean feature_063: responders 5.78, non-responders 6.15 (difference -0.37). Mann-Whitney p=2.0e-6.

Only feature_011 (p=1.4e-32), feature_063 (p=2.0e-6), feature_099 (p=3.5e-5), and feature_092 (p=8.4e-3) reach p<0.05. The remaining 31 continuous features (e.g., feature_055, feature_070, feature_044, feature_121, feature_028) are all p>0.08, supporting that most lab values are not predictive in isolation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 7 multi-level integer features (feature_051, feature_026, feature_033, feature_064, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042), feature_051 (3 levels: 0/1/2) has the strongest ordinal relationship with objective_response, with response decreasing monotonically as the level increases. Plausibly an ECOG performance status surrogate.
The remaining multi-level features (feature_026, feature_033, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042, feature_064) show NO significant overall association with response.

analyses.result_summary: Response by feature_051 level: 0 -> 0.211 (n=17557); 1 -> 0.157 (n=24962); 2 -> 0.111 (n=7481). Strict monotonic decrease across the three levels. chi-square p=2.9e-93. By far the strongest single predictor in the dataset.

feature_026 p=0.103, feature_033 p=0.207, feature_018 p=0.636, feature_045 p=0.195, feature_042 p=0.912, feature_064 p=0.824. None significant. Response rates are essentially flat across the levels of these six features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the four free-text categorical features, race (feature_123) shows a small but statistically significant association with response, while smoking status (feature_057), histology (feature_043), and insurance (feature_005) do NOT.
White patients have a LOWER objective_response rate than non-white patients (race disparity in response in this dataset).

analyses.result_summary: feature_057 (smoking) p=0.269, feature_043 (histology) p=0.078, feature_005 (insurance) p=0.232, feature_123 (race) p=0.045. Only race reaches conventional significance, and even that is borderline.

Response by race: asian 0.177 (n=2979), black 0.178 (n=6008), hispanic 0.172 (n=7392), other 0.185 (n=1014), white 0.165 (n=32607). Collapsed white vs non-white: 0.1655 vs 0.1754, chi-square p=0.0050. White patients have ~1 percentage point lower response than non-white. Direction supports h12.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age (feature_078, distribution 30-90, mean ~65) does NOT differ between responders and non-responders.
Smoking status (feature_057) has no main effect on response within the cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_078: responders 65.03 yr, non-responders 64.98 yr. t-test p=0.668. No association with response.
Response by smoking status: current 0.172 (n=14974), former 0.168 (n=27567), never 0.164 (n=7459). chi-square p=0.269. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression that simultaneously adjusts for the strongest screened variables (feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_076, feature_112, feature_011, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_051), each of feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, and feature_051 retains its independent direction and statistical significance.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds coefficients (z, p): feature_013 = -0.302 (z=-12.3, p<1e-30); feature_067 = -0.259 (z=-8.9, p<1e-15); feature_011 (per SD) = -0.153 (z=-12.1, p<1e-30); feature_051 (per ordinal level) = -0.381 (z=-20.8, p<1e-90). All four retain direction and high significance after mutual adjustment, supporting independent contributions. feature_006, _007, _039 each remain weakly positive (p=0.001-0.03). Pseudo-R^2 = 0.020.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive 2-way interaction between feature_006 and feature_007: their joint presence raises response above what additivity predicts.
There is a positive 2-way interaction between feature_006 and feature_039.
There is a positive 2-way interaction between feature_007 and feature_039.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.149, z=2.86, p=0.0043. Positive synergistic interaction confirmed.
Interaction coefficient = +0.184, z=3.53, p=0.0004. Positive synergistic interaction confirmed.
Interaction coefficient = +0.146, z=3.04, p=0.0024. Positive synergistic interaction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three pairwise positive interactions among feature_006, feature_007, feature_039 are actually driven by a single 3-way interaction: response is elevated only in the cell where all three are simultaneously =1, and is essentially flat in every other cell. The 3-way interaction term will be highly significant after fitting all 2-way and 3-way terms together.

analyses.result_summary: Cell response rates: (0,0,0) 0.161 (n=9882); (0,0,1) 0.162 (n=9920); (0,1,0) 0.165 (n=7963); (0,1,1) 0.168 (n=7977); (1,0,0) 0.169 (n=3932); (1,0,1) 0.169 (n=3895); (1,1,0) 0.165 (n=3215); (1,1,1) 0.230 (n=3216). Only the all-three-=1 cell stands out (~6 pp above baseline). In the full model with main, 2-way and 3-way terms, the 3-way term coefficient = +0.419 (z=3.97, p=7.3e-5). The three pairwise terms become non-significant. Strongly suggests a triple-combination effect (e.g., a regimen marker requiring all three components).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with all three of feature_006=1 AND feature_007=1 AND feature_039=1 (a 'triple-positive' indicator, prevalence 6.4%) have a significantly higher response rate than the rest of the cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive (n=3216, 6.4%) response rate = 0.230; all others (n=46784) = 0.165. Univariate logit coefficient = +0.414 (OR ~1.51), z=9.46, p=5.4e-20. Risk difference +6.5 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (resistance biomarker) and feature_067 (resistance biomarker) act independently and ADDITIVELY: patients positive for both have the lowest response, those positive for neither the highest, with no significant 2-way interaction between them.

analyses.result_summary: Cell response rates: (013=0, 067=0) 0.207 (n=13223); (0,1) 0.165 (n=4412); (1,0) 0.161 (n=24193); (1,1) 0.132 (n=8172). Effect appears additive: each marker individually drops response ~4 pp; joint presence drops it ~7 pp. Interaction term coef=+0.039, p=0.50 (not significant). Supports additive (not multiplicative) effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on response is QUALITATIVELY HOMOGENEOUS across racial subgroups (white, hispanic, black, asian, other) — i.e., it is not a spurious effect driven by one ethnic group.

analyses.result_summary: Within-race effect of feature_013 (rr feature_013=0 -> rr feature_013=1, p): white 0.194 -> 0.150, p=1.0e-24; hispanic 0.194 -> 0.159, p=1.1e-4; black 0.208 -> 0.161, p=5.4e-6; asian 0.192 -> 0.169, p=0.124 (NS but same direction); other 0.224 -> 0.166, p=0.030. Direction is NEGATIVE in all 5 racial subgroups; magnitude is similar (~3-6 pp). Effect is homogeneous.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-positive (feature_006 & feature_007 & feature_039) response benefit persists across all three levels of feature_051 (performance status surrogate), with the absolute benefit largest in the lowest-PS group.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051=0: triple_pos rr=0.270 vs 0.207 (delta +6.3 pp); feature_051=1: 0.213 vs 0.153 (+6.0 pp); feature_051=2: 0.187 vs 0.105 (+8.2 pp). Triple-positive provides similar absolute benefit at every PS level. Interaction term feature_051 x triple_pos coef=+0.126, p=0.056 (borderline, suggesting modestly larger relative benefit at higher PS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-positive benefit is preserved across all racial subgroups (no race-restricted regimen effect).

analyses.code: for race: triple_pos rr vs others
analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive vs others response by race: white 0.226 vs 0.161; hispanic 0.219 vs 0.168; black 0.255 vs 0.172; asian 0.235 vs 0.173; other 0.271 vs 0.180. Triple-positive provides 5-9 pp absolute benefit in every racial subgroup; direction consistent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (continuous, response-decreasing) acts approximately additively with feature_013 — patients who are HIGH on feature_011 AND positive for feature_013 have especially low response, but with no multiplicative interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Cell response rates: f011 low & feature_013=0: 0.213 (n=8691); low & 13=1: 0.170 (n=16099); high & 13=0: 0.180 (n=8944); high & 13=1: 0.138 (n=16266). Drop ~4 pp per factor, additively. Logistic interaction term coef=-0.013 (z=-0.68, p=0.50). No significant interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (performance-status surrogate) does NOT modify the effect of resistance-biomarker feature_013 on response: the biomarker effect size is constant across PS levels.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_051=0: rr 0.242 (013=0) vs 0.194 (013=1), delta -4.8 pp; within feature_051=1: 0.187 vs 0.140, delta -4.7 pp; within feature_051=2: 0.122 vs 0.104, delta -1.8 pp. Logistic interaction coef = +0.013, p=0.73. No significant interaction; biomarker effect is stable across PS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Histology (feature_043 adenocarcinoma vs squamous) does NOT modify the effects of feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051, or the triple-positive indicator (no histology-restricted effect modification).

analyses.code: for var: Logit(y ~ var + adeno + var*adeno)
analyses.result_summary: Histology x feature_013 interaction p=0.40; x feature_067 p=0.66; x feature_011 p=0.73; x feature_051 p=0.22; x feature_006 p=0.62; x feature_007 p=0.57; x feature_039 p=0.58; x feature_099 p=0.41. None significant. Effects are histology-uniform.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking status (feature_057) does NOT modify the effect of feature_013 or feature_011, indicating these resistance markers behave the same in current/former/never smokers.

analyses.code: for var: Logit(y ~ var + smk_current + smk_never + var*smk_current + var*smk_never)
analyses.result_summary: Smoking x feature_013: inter_curr p=0.82, inter_never p=0.42. Smoking x feature_011: inter_curr p=0.17, inter_never p=0.57. Smoking x feature_006: inter_curr p=0.59, inter_never p=0.46. Smoking x feature_051: inter_curr p=0.94, inter_never p=0.33. None significant. Effects are smoking-status uniform.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_099 has a small POSITIVE association with response (higher values associated with higher response), but the effect is much weaker than feature_011 and is independent of it.
Continuous feature_092 also has a weak positive association with response, robust to adjustment for feature_011.

analyses.code: ttest then Logit(y ~ feature_011_z + feature_063_z + feature_099_z + feature_092_z)
analyses.result_summary: Univariate: mean feature_099 0.023 higher among responders, p=3.5e-5. Adjusted (in multivariable model with feature_011/063/092): coef per SD = +0.046, z=3.85, p=1.2e-4. Direction positive, robust.
feature_092 per SD coefficient = +0.037, z=3.06, p=0.002. Independent of feature_011 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_005) has NO main effect on objective_response after accounting for the noise in this 4-level field.

analyses.result_summary: Response by insurance: medicaid 0.161 (n=6999), medicare 0.168 (n=21178), private 0.172 (n=19823), uninsured 0.170 (n=2000). chi-square p=0.232. No significant association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 has a positive main effect on response that is NOT just a sex-by-histology confound: it remains positive within both adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.

analyses.result_summary: In adenocarcinoma: feature_007=0 rr=0.162, =1 rr=0.173; in squamous: =0 rr=0.167, =1 rr=0.182. Direction positive in both histologies (delta ~1.0-1.5 pp). No histology x feature_007 interaction (p=0.57).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The bottom-tier binary features that screened with p>0.5 (feature_001, feature_002, feature_037, feature_102, feature_115, feature_122, feature_034, feature_040, feature_050, feature_025) truly have NO association with response and not even a weak signal — these are noise variables.

analyses.code: rr by feature for the 10 'least significant' binaries
analyses.result_summary: Risk differences for these 10 features range from -0.0045 to +0.0017 (i.e., < ±0.5 pp). All p > 0.84. Consistent with no true effect; these features can be treated as null/noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all key predictors and the 3-way interaction are placed in a single multivariable logistic model (feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039 + 2-way and 3-way among 006/007/039, plus feature_063, feature_099, feature_092), the model retains the directionally consistent effects: feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051, feature_063 all NEGATIVE; feature_099, feature_092, and the 3-way 006*007*039 all POSITIVE.

analyses.code: Final Logit with 14 covariates including 3-way interaction
analyses.result_summary: Final model coefficients: feature_013 -0.303 (p<1e-30); feature_067 -0.260 (p<1e-15); feature_011/SD -0.153 (p<1e-30); feature_051 -0.382 (p<1e-90); feature_063/SD -0.046 (p=4e-4); feature_099/SD +0.047 (p=1e-4); feature_092/SD +0.037 (p=0.002); 3-way 006*007*039 = +0.419 (p=7.3e-5). Main effects of feature_006/007/039 alone are non-significant once the 3-way is in the model. Pseudo-R^2 = 0.021; LLR p = 1e-191.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 prevalence (~65%) is NOT confounded with race — feature_013 is similarly distributed across racial subgroups (so the response-effect cannot be explained by race composition).

analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 prevalence by race: hispanic 0.642, white 0.650, black 0.638, asian 0.649, other 0.661. Range only 0.02; well within sampling noise. No confounding with race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesizing all iterations: the cohort's response to therapy is governed primarily by (a) a performance-status-like ordinal feature (feature_051), (b) two independent binary resistance markers (feature_013, feature_067), (c) one continuous lab marker that increases with non-response (feature_011), and (d) a triple-combination indicator (feature_006 AND feature_007 AND feature_039) that lifts response by ~6.5 pp. Other clinical features (age, smoking, insurance, histology) and the bulk of binary/continuous features are not independently predictive at the n=50000 level.

analyses.code: Composite summary across all multivariable and subgroup analyses
analyses.result_summary: Final integrated picture: 4 strong independent predictors (feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011) and 1 strong 3-way interaction (feature_006*feature_007*feature_039) collectively explain the small but real predictable component of objective_response in this NSCLC cohort. The total model pseudo-R^2 is ~0.021, indicating most of response variance remains unexplained (consistent with real-world oncology cohorts). The race signal is small (~1 pp) and does NOT confound any of the principal effects (feature_013 prevalence is uniform across race). No effect modification was detected for histology, smoking, insurance, age, or PS on the principal predictors. The triple-positive (feature_006 & 007 & 039) benefit appears robust across subgroups (race, PS) and is consistent with a combination-regimen-like mechanism in the data.
